Package org.areslib.command

Class HeadingLockCommand

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.command.Command
org.areslib.command.HeadingLockCommand
public class HeadingLockCommand extends Command
A command that stabilizes the robot's heading when the driver isn't actively rotating.

When the driver releases the rotation stick, the command locks the current heading and applies PID correction to prevent drift during strafing. When the driver starts rotating again, the lock is released and raw input passes through.

This is drivetrain-agnostic — it works with any drive type by accepting suppliers and consumers. 


 new HeadingLockCommand(
     () -> -gamepad1.right_stick_x,  // rotation input
     () -> imu.getYaw(),             // current heading (radians)
     omega -> driveOmega = omega,    // omega output
     0.5, 0.0, 0.02,                // PID gains
     0.05                            // deadband
 );

  • Constructor Details

    • HeadingLockCommand

      public HeadingLockCommand(DoubleSupplier rotationInput, DoubleSupplier gyroHeading, DoubleConsumer omegaOutput, double kP, double kI, double kD, double deadband)
      Constructs a HeadingLockCommand.
      Parameters:
      rotationInput - Supplier for the driver's rotation joystick input (-1 to 1).
      gyroHeading - Supplier for the current gyro heading in radians.
      omegaOutput - Consumer that receives the corrected omega output.
      kP - Heading PID proportional gain.
      kI - Heading PID integral gain.
      kD - Heading PID derivative gain.
      deadband - The rotation input deadband below which heading lock engages.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Description copied from class: Command
      The initial subroutine of a command. Called once when the command is initially scheduled.

    • execute

      public void execute()
      Description copied from class: Command
      The main body of a command. Called repeatedly while the command is scheduled.

    • isLocked

      public boolean isLocked()
      Returns whether the heading lock is currently active.
      Returns:
      The current value.
